results.finalAnswer:
ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: routes - versioned API contracts live in api_v1.php, api_v2.php, and api_v3.php, so this is where request URLs and controller targets first split
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http - Kernel.php, middleware, controllers, and FormRequests define auth, throttling, logging, validation, and request normalization before business logic runs
SUBSYSTEM: app/Components/Api/Application/Services/ApplicationService.php - application create/update/prefill flows converge here after controllers, so request-shape changes should be traced through this write path

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Kernel.php - global and grouped middleware here control CORS, auth, throttling, logging, and request context before controllers run
SUBSYSTEM: routes/ - versioned API route files (api_v1.php, api_v2.php, api_v3.php) define the public request surface and show which middleware/controller each endpoint hits
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Requests - FormRequests are the safest boundary for authorization, validation, and request payload shaping before thin controllers hand off to services

ENTRYPOINT: public/index.php
SUBSYSTEM: app/Providers/RouteServiceProvider.php - maps api/api-v2/api-v3 route files and rate limits, so versioned request flow starts here
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Kernel.php - defines the global and api middleware stack, including token auth, throttling, Sentry context, and request logging
SUBSYSTEM: app/Http/Requests - FormRequest classes handle authorization, validation, and input normalization before thin controllers hand off to services
